title: quercus × beaumontiana
intro: Quercus × beaumontiana, a hybrid of the white oak (Quercus alba) and the swamp white oak (Quercus bicolor), is a deciduous tree native to the southeastern United States. The leaves of the Quercus × beaumontiana plant have been used in herbal medicine due to their astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which can aid in treating wounds, sore throats, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Quercus × beaumontiana include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and promoting overall well-being. Quercus × beaumontiana can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, allowing for various herbal preparations to be used for medicinal purposes.
